differentials.name: maculopapular rash + ulcers of soft palate
differentials.raw: maculopapular rash + ulcers of soft palate (occasionally)

context: √  diffusely normal mucosal background √  one / more giant ovoid flat ulcers (up to several cm in size) near gastroesophageal junction √  discrete small superficial ulcers indistinguishable from herpes esophagitis (uncommon) ◊  2nd most common cause of opportunistic infection! Organism:   Herpes simplex virus type I (DNA core virus) secreted in saliva of 2% of healthy population Age:  15-30 years; usually males Predisposed:   immunocompromised patient with HIV, organ transplant, and on chemotherapy; may occur in immunocompetent patient Location:   midesophagus (level of left main bronchus) √
